channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A stretch of a North Carolina highway that collapsed during Helene is about to reopen
item.description: A stretch of Interstate 40 through the western North Carolina mountains is reopening to traffic this weekend, months after Hurricane Helene's historic flooding collapsed portions of the road, partially restoring the major travel connection with eastern Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Washington man arrested for stealing $8M fishing boat with crew still on board, officials say
item.description: The suspect allegedly tried to steal a different boat earlier that morning, but its crew "ran him off the boat," police said.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, KRON4

item.title: Vice President JD Vance, a Catholic, acknowledges the pope's criticism of US immigration crackdown
item.description: Vice President JD Vance acknowledged Pope Francis’ criticism of the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigration, without responding to any of its specifics or to the pontiff’s apparent criticism of Vance’s own deployment of Catholic tradition to justify such policies.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Federal agencies plan second email asking workers for weekly accomplishments
item.description: Some agencies plan to send a second Saturday email to federal workers asking them for bullet points on what they did this week, a sign at least some departments plan to continue with the controversial practice ignited by Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: Oscar-winner Gene Hackman and wife Betsy Arakawa may have been dead for days or weeks, sheriff says
item.description: Authorities investigating the deaths of Oscar-winner Gene Hackman and his wife are waiting for more details from their autopsies to determine why they died.
item.dc:creator: Susan Montoya Bryan And John Seewer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: To her, Hurricane Helene debris isn’t trash. It is full of memories
item.description: U.S. National Guard Capt. Jill Holtz spends much of her free time in Swannanoa, North Carolina, searching for items lost from Hurricane Helene's severe flooding a few months ago.
item.dc:creator: Makiya Seminera, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Author Robert Harris on 'Conclave' success ahead of Sunday's Oscars
item.description: LONDON (AP) — “Conclave” author Robert Harris isn’t planning to stay up and watch the Oscars. The British writer will be in a different time zone. Plus, Harris would rather wait to see how the movie, which is up for eight Academy Awards including best picture, does Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: HILARY FOX, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US lobster catch drops as crustaceans migrate to colder Canadian waters
item.description: SCARBOROUGH, Maine (AP) — The U.S. lobster industry's catch keeps sliding as fishermen contend with the northward migration of the valuable crustaceans. The industry is based mostly in Maine, where lobsters are both a cultural signifier and the backbone of the coastal economy.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Midwest winters are changing. So is the ancient sport of falconry
item.description: For falconers who hunt small animals like rabbits and grouse with wild birds of prey, changing Midwest winters are also changing the sport they love.
item.dc:creator: Melina Walling, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pro-Israel influencer says Bibas memorial in NYC 'brought out the worst' in antisemites
item.description: Pro-Israel influencer Zach Sage Fox set out to honor Ariel and Kfir Bibas with a memorial in Times Square, but he says he was met with "some of the worst antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Leonard Peltier remains defiant in AP interview, maintaining innocence and vowing continued activism
item.description: BELCOURT, N.D. (AP) — More than 50 years after a shootout on the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation landed him in federal prison, Leonard Peltier remains defiant.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kristin Crowley appeals Los Angeles mayor's decision to terminate her as LAFD chief
item.description: Former Los Angeles Fire Chief Kristin Crowley announced on Thursday she is appealing Mayor Karen Bass’ decision to fire her a week ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iowa gives final approval to a bill removing gender identity protections despite protests
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Iowa lawmakers became the first in the nation to approve legislation removing gender identity protections from the state’s civil rights code Thursday, despite massive protests by opponents who say it could expose transgender people to discrimination in numerous areas of life.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: King Charles invites Trump to UK for a state visit
item.description: King Charles III has invited President Trump for a state visit to the United Kingdom, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer told the president during their meeting Thursday in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Former sheriff ‘flabbergasted’ by alleged gun seizure incident
item.description: “It sounds like robbery,” Wilson’s attorney, Bobby Bailey, said. “To go to a person’s personal job and to take his personal items; to me, that’s robbery.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: GOP Sen. Mike Lee suggests letting ‘pirates’ take on cartels on the border
item.description: “Privateers is what they’re called,” he said. “Letters of marque and reprisal are authorized under Article 1, Section 8, Clause 10 of the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Meet the only 2 passengers from the 'private' Southwest flight out of Utah
item.description: "It was so cool. It was a really fun experience," Natalie Wolfley said. "Obviously, once in a lifetime, probably -- I don't know if I'll ever have that kind of experience again.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, NewsNation

item.title: Man arrested in Nashville for impersonating law enforcement
item.description: According to police, 31-year-old Leart Bytyqi was identified as the man who tried to pull over a driver by flashing a badge out the car window.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Guerry, NewsNation

item.title: Landlord charged after allegedly leaving tenant without water for 54 days
item.description: The water was turned off from Dec. 6 until Jan. 2, as Yopp refused to have a plumber come by to fix it.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Lepore, NewsNation

item.title: Students escape school bus fire in Ohio
item.description: An emergency broke out Thursday morning on a school bus carrying children in Cleveland Heights, Ohio. An unexpected blaze — requiring a quick evacuation.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NOAA begins firing hundreds of staffers
item.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) began the process of firing hundreds of probationary workers Thursday, the latest mass job cuts within federal agencies, three sources with knowledge of internal operations told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to save your video: Facebook to automatically delete live videos after 30 days
item.description: Facebook is changing its live video feature, allowing users to keep their videos for 30 days after publishing, after which they will be automatically deleted from Meta servers.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Thompson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Economic blackout protest underway Friday
item.description: (AP) — A grassroots organization is encouraging U.S. residents not to spend any money Friday as an act of “economic resistance” to protest what the group's founder sees as the malign influence of billionaires, big corporations and both major political parties on the lives of working Americans.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Texas says this doctor illegally treated trans youth. He says he followed the law
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (AP) — On the Texas border, Dr. Hector Granados treats children with diabetes at his El Paso clinics and makes hospital rounds under the shadow of accusations that have thrown his career into jeopardy: providing care to transgender youth. In what's believed to be a U.S.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pitches 'gold card' as replacement for EB5 investor visa
item.description: The Trump administration will be revamping the U.S. investor visa, increasing the funds required to secure the status while dubbing the new option a "gold card.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge dismisses racketeering indictment against New Jersey Democratic power broker, co-defendants
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — A state judge on Wednesday agreed to toss out racketeering charges against New Jersey Democratic power broker George Norcross and those charged alongside him.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The US Christian population has declined for years. A new survey shows that drop leveling off
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The number of Americans who identify as Christian has declined steadily for years, but that drop shows signs of slowing, according to a new survey Wednesday from the Pew Research Center. The Religious Landscape Study finds 62% of U.S. adults call themselves Christians.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Adnan Syed's murder conviction still stands as he seeks sentence reduction in 'Serial' case
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Despite documented problems with the evidence against him and an earlier request from prosecutors to clear his record, Adnan Syed will remain convicted of murder, according to court papers filed Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Over 20 staffers in office overtaken by DOGE resign, say they won't 'jeopardize' essential government systems
item.description: The staffers said they won’t use their technical skills to “jeopardize” Americans’ data and “compromise” essential government systems.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nearly 200K sign petition to revoke Musk's Canadian citizenship
item.description: The petition calls on Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau to cancel Musk's citizenship and Canadian passport.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: Chris Jasper of the Isley Brothers dies at 73
item.description: Chris Jasper, a member of the Isley Brothers and Isley-Jasper-Isley, passed away on Sunday. He was 73 years old.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises: Vatican
item.description: “In the morning, after receiving the Eucharist, he resumed work activities,” the Vatican statement said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
